guidance.seedAdvice: Use certified seed or clean farm-saved seed. Treat seed with beejamrit before sowing.
guidance.rotationAdvice: Follow with moong or fodder legume to support soil nitrogen and break disease cycles.
guidance.subsidyNote: Check state agriculture office, PM-KISAN eligibility, and natural farming scheme enrollment.

guidance.seedAdvice: Choose drought-tolerant bajra variety for light soils. Sow after useful rain and keep seed shallow.
guidance.rotationAdvice: Rotate with gram, mustard, or moong to reduce pest pressure and improve income spread.
guidance.subsidyNote: Ask the local agriculture department about seed mini-kits and millet promotion support.

guidance.seedAdvice: Use short-duration moong where monsoon window is uncertain. Treat with rhizobium and beejamrit.
guidance.rotationAdvice: Good after wheat and before mustard; helps soil nitrogen and gives quick cash flow.
guidance.subsidyNote: Pulse seed support may be available through state seed outlets or Krishi Vigyan Kendra programs.

guidance.seedAdvice: Use low seed rate with line sowing. Avoid waterlogging and keep early aphid monitoring.
guidance.rotationAdvice: Works after bajra or moong. Avoid repeating mustard on the same plot every year.
guidance.subsidyNote: Verify oilseed seed subsidy and soil-health-card recommendations locally.

guidance.seedAdvice: Pick locally approved seed and maintain refuge practices where applicable. Monitor whitefly weekly.
guidance.rotationAdvice: Rotate with wheat, gram, or fodder. Avoid continuous cotton in pest-prone fields.
guidance.subsidyNote: Confirm seed rules and crop insurance dates with the agriculture office before purchase.

financeRules.homeInputSavingsNote: Cost falls when manure, cow urine, crop residue, and seed are available on-farm.
financeRules.decisionRule: Keep one season of input buffer before expanding natural farming to all acres.
